ai_strategy_lead.name: Institute for Physical AI
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: Purdue University has a public AI posture anchored in Institute for Physical AI. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Purdue University publishes AI policy or guidance with a comprehensive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: Institute for Physical AI
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Purdue University is evidenced through Institute for Physical AI and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI policy and guidance

investment.description: No public dollar amount disclosed for this initiative.

collaborations.description: No external collaboration identified in the cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets institutional guardrails for classroom, research, staff and administrative use.

initiatives.title: Variate AI assessment generator and Charlie AI teaching assistant
initiatives.description: Purdue Engineering reports development of Variate, an AI-powered engine that helps instructors generate individualized assessments, alongside Charlie, a Socratic-style AI teaching assistant. The tools are framed around academic-integrity pressure from large language models and around giving every student tailored problem variants.
initiatives.significance: This is a public, engineering-scale example of assessment redesign rather than only AI-use policy guidance.

investment.description: Public sources describe the tools and teaching use case but do not disclose a product budget.

collaborations.description: The cited source presents the work as Purdue-developed instructional technology.

initiatives.policy_implications: The initiative responds to generative-AI integrity concerns by redesigning assessments and feedback paths.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Purdue's NSF-funded Anvil system is a named AI/HPC compute resource with 1,000 AMD EPYC compute nodes and 16 GPU nodes, each with four NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs, for a disclosed total of 64 GPUs.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and inclusive AI access

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: Purdue University has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is Institute for Physical AI, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

search_queries: Purdue Anvil supercomputer NSF 10 million GPUs A100 official
Purdue University AI assessment redesign generative AI teaching assignments official
Purdue University generative AI campus platform Microsoft Copilot official
gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
Anvil GPU capacity and NSF funding are public, but GenAI Studio and Microsoft Copilot user counts, license terms, and contract values were not disclosed in the reviewed sources.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
